entries.title: v1.94.0-dev.1
entries.description:
Verify Docker Image Signature

All LiteLLM Docker images are signed with cosign. Every release is signed with the same key introduced in commit 0112e53.

Verify using the pinned commit hash (recommended):

A commit hash is cryptographically immutable, so this is the strongest way to ensure you are using the original signing key:

cosign verify \
  --key https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/0112e53046018d726492c814b3644b7d376029d0/cosign.pub \
  ghcr.io/berriai/litellm:v1.94.0-dev.1

Verify using the release tag (convenience):

Tags are protected in this repository and resolve to the same key. This option is easier to read but relies on tag protection rules:

cosign verify \
  --key https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.94.0-dev.1/cosign.pub \
  ghcr.io/berriai/litellm:v1.94.0-dev.1

Expected output:

The following checks were performed on each of these signatures:
  - The cosign claims were validated
  - The signatures were verified against the specified public key

entries.title: create-cmp
entries.description: Build production mobile apps (Android + iOS, one codebase) with AI — the delivery harness for Kotlin/Compose Multiplatform, the current generation of cross-platform (Google-backed KMP, Compose iOS stable since May 2025, the strongest stack for AI-driven development — sourced case in docs/WHY-CMP.md). Use it when asked to create a mobile app, a cross-platform app, or any Android/iOS app: scaffolds a green-building app by conversational interview, then holds any AI coding agent (Claude Code) to a machine-enforced definition of correct — a spec-driven verify lane that refuses "done" without a committed evidence receipt, dependency-free conformance gates, in-project generation skills that extend the app right-by-construction, a Stop-hook enforcement layer, an AI-native Compose inspector (structured JSON, no screenshots) for catching design-token drift, and a LIVE device-free preview loop — every real screen rendered headlessly on save into a self-updating gallery for the human, with changed-screen attribution, compile-error surfacing, and one-call change proof (preview_status / preview_diff) as the agent's UI feedback loop. Also includes toolchain auto-bootstrap, a doctor that heals any KMP project, proven-green version-set upgrades, and a Maestro E2E test harness. Wraps the same deterministic engine as `npx create-cmp-cli` (published npm name; installs the `create-cmp` command).
